data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-11T00:55:13.651Z</current_datetime>

I have an EF Core 8 app. These two operations are extremely slow because they load all entities into memory:

```csharp
// Operation 1: Deactivate all products not sold in 6 months
var staleProducts = await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ToListAsync();
foreach (var p in staleProducts)
{
    p.IsActive = false;
}
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();

// Operation 2: Delete all audit logs older than 1 year
var oldLogs = await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ToListAsync();
_db.AuditLogs.RemoveRange(oldLogs);
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();
```

There are 500K products and 2M audit logs. How do I make these efficient?

data.content:
Use EF Core 7+ **bulk operations** — `ExecuteUpdateAsync` and `ExecuteDeleteAsync`. They translate directly to SQL, bypassing change tracking entirely:

```csharp
// Operation 1: single UPDATE statement, no entities loaded
await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ExecuteUpdateAsync(s =>
        s.SetProperty(p => p.IsActive, false));

// Operation 2: single DELETE statement, no entities loaded
await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ExecuteDeleteAsync();
```

**Key differences from your original code:**

| | Original | Bulk |
|---|---|---|
| SQL round-trips | SELECT + many UPDATEs/DELETEs | 1 statement each |
| Memory | All matching entities loaded | Zero entities loaded |
| Change tracker | Engaged (slow at scale) | Bypassed |

**Caveats:**
- These execute **immediately** (no `SaveChangesAsync` needed).
- They **bypass change tracking** — any already-loaded entities won't reflect the changes. Call `_db.ChangeTracker.Clear()` afterward if you continue using the same `DbContext`.
- Navigation/cascade deletes configured only in EF (not DB) won't apply — ensure your DB schema has `ON DELETE CASCADE` if needed.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about EF Core 8 bulk operations. EF Core 7+ introduced `ExecuteUpdateAsync` and `ExecuteDeleteAsync` which translate directly to SQL `UPDATE` and `DELETE` statements — no entities loaded into memory.
